newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. Only items: third-period restricted stock incentive vesting for 69 core employees (324,450 shares, ~CNY 415M, listed for circulation June 8 - trivial vs. ~$190B cap) and a notice for the 2026 second extraordinary shareholders' meeting. Known backdrop unchanged: Q1 revenue CNY 19.5B (+192% YoY), ~40%+ global 800G share and 50-70% 1.6T share, 6.4T NPO / 12.8T XPO in preparation. Today's +2.17% move is flow/drift, not news-driven.

sources.label: Jiemian - Innolight unlocks CNY 415M restricted stock for 69 core employees (June 4-8)
STCN - Innolight employee incentive shares to list for circulation
Sina Finance - 300308 company announcements (incl. 2026 second EGM notice)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved it down for the discovered/crowded setup after an ~85% YTD run; since June 6 there is no genuinely new information, only an immaterial incentive-share unlock and another +2.17% of newsless drift, so a second consecutive demotion is not warranted. Small negative delta reflects continued rerating without fresh evidence eroding residual alpha at rank 112.
verdicts.bull: Dominant 800G (~40%+ share) and 1.6T (50-70% share) franchise with Q1 revenue up 192% and 6.4T NPO / 12.8T XPO pipeline keeps it the cash-flow king of AI optics; if 1.6T volumes beat in H2, estimates still have upside.
verdicts.neutral: The franchise is excellent but fully discovered at ~$190B cap and +89% YTD; on an alpha list rewarding hidden bottleneck names, it correctly sits deep in the table regardless of quality.
verdicts.bear: Crowded ownership plus CPO architecture transition risk: if hyperscalers shift to co-packaged optics faster than expected, the pluggable-module moat compresses just as the multiple peaks; insider/incentive share unlocks add marginal supply.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H2 2026 1.6T shipment ramp and Q2 earnings beating elevated expectations; tangible 6.4T NPO/XPO design wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating de-rating - any hyperscaler capex digestion or faster-than-expected CPO substitution hits a crowded, +89% YTD position hard.
